steps.label: cause
steps.text: Vines are planted on very steep Douro slopes where an unmodified surface is difficult to cultivate.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Terraces create narrower workable planting surfaces and can manage access and erosion, while preserving slope exposure.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Viticulture becomes possible on steep sites that receive strong solar exposure but remain costly and difficult to work.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: terraces convert steep slopes into plantable, accessible vineyard surfaces.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
